configure.note: For LOCAL installations, EchoBird's App Manager handles model configuration automatically. This section is for REMOTE server deployments via Mother Agent (SSH).

field_mapping.description: EchoBird sends model data in the '[Attached models for deployment]' block. Format: '- <name> (model: <modelId>, baseUrl: <url>, anthropicUrl: <url>, apiKey: <key>)'. Map to OpenClaw as follows:
field_mapping.attachment_model_field: → OpenClaw model id AND primary ref suffix: eb_custom/<model>
field_mapping.attachment_anthropicUrl: → OpenClaw provider baseUrl when using anthropic-messages (Option B). PREFER this over baseUrl when present.
field_mapping.attachment_baseUrl: → OpenClaw provider baseUrl when using openai-completions (Option A). Only use if anthropicUrl is absent.
field_mapping.attachment_apiKey: → Store in env block as EB_CUSTOM_API_KEY, reference in provider as ${EB_CUSTOM_API_KEY}

configure.decision_rule: If the attachment has anthropicUrl → Option B (api: anthropic-messages, baseUrl = anthropicUrl). If only baseUrl → Option A (api: openai-completions, baseUrl = baseUrl).
steps: 1. Parse the '[Attached models for deployment]' block to extract: name, model (=modelId), baseUrl, anthropicUrl, apiKey.
2. Apply decision_rule to choose api type and which URL to use as provider baseUrl.
3. Run merge_script below with the extracted values.

4. Restart gateway: pkill -f 'openclaw gateway' || true && sleep 1 && export PATH="$HOME/.npm-global/bin:$HOME/.local/bin:$PATH" && nohup openclaw gateway --allow-unconfigured > /tmp/openclaw.log 2>&1 &
configure.after_configure: pkill -f 'openclaw gateway' || true && sleep 1 && export PATH="$HOME/.npm-global/bin:$HOME/.local/bin:$PATH" && nohup openclaw gateway --allow-unconfigured > /tmp/openclaw.log 2>&1 &
